rationale: The answer starts with a brake and refuses a new door-access channel as the default, so it partially wakes up the anti-spiral behavior. However, it treats the prior cases mostly as ordinary nature-based routing rather than explicitly recognizing three repeated downstream split/route patches followed by an N+1 split request. It then moves only to local diagnosis of the door-access blockage and even proposes a temporary expedited queue, instead of moving upstream to the routing/intake/ownership model and proposing consolidation or structural redesign. Thus it is a partial warning, not a full brake.
